$comment: C 6.2.3 tag namespace (closes the tag-namespace residue of D-CSUBSET-DECL-GRAMMAR-LOW-RESIDUES). `typedef struct Pair { int a; int b; } Pair;` puts the tag `Pair` (Tag namespace) and the typedef alias `Pair` (Ordinary namespace) under ONE spelling — previously S_RedeclaredSymbol. main() resolves the alias (`Pair p;`, Ordinary leaf arm) AND the tag (`struct Pair q;`, the type-position tag-reference early-arm MF-1) to the SAME struct type; field arithmetic 40+2 = 42 proves both namespaces resolve independently. RED-ON-DISABLE: without the Tag namespace the typedef line fails the compile loud and the example never links. The optimizedPipelines arm re-runs the full release composition (Inlining included) — struct member-access + the tag/alias resolution must survive optimization.
